core_content.markdown:
Matrix Form of the Linear System (1). (continued)

We assume that the coefficients $a_{j k}$ are not all $\mathbf{z e r o},$ sot that A is not tazero matrix.Note that t x has n components,whereas 

$$\tilde{\mathbf{A}}=\left[\begin{matrix}{a_{_{11}}}&{\cdots}&{a_{_{1n}}}&{\vdots}&{b_{_{1}}}\\ {\cdot}&{\cdots}&{\cdot}&{\vdots}&{\cdot}\\ {\cdot}&{\cdots}&{\cdot}&{\vdots}&{\cdot}\\ {a_{_{m1}}}&{\cdots}&{a_{_{m n}}}&{\vdots}&{b_{_{m}}}\end{matrix}\right]$$

is called the augmented matrix of the system (1).The dashed vertical line could be omitted,as we shall  do later.It is merely a1reminder that the last column of $\tilde{\mathbf{A}}$ did not come from matrix Ａbut came fromv vector b. Thus, we augmented the matrix A.

rec_texts: Advanced Engineering Mathematics, 10/e by Edwin Kreyszig
